data.title: A whale closed its long position in Ethereum, incurring a loss of $10.28 million, and then opened a short position with 6x leverage.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale closed its 6x leveraged long position in Ethereum, incurring a loss of $10.28 million. However, it subsequently opened a 6x leveraged short position, currently holding approximately 3,022 ETH at a liquidation price of $3,018.4.

data.title: Investors flee corporate bonds as AI infrastructure borrowing spikes
data.description:
Investors are pulling money out of bonds fast as huge AI-related borrowing and growing stress in private credit shake confidence across global markets, according to reporting from Reuters. Lenders who usually back top-rated companies are now stepping aside because fresh debt sales from Big Tech and trouble inside private credit funds are raising the risk […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/investors-flee-corporate-bonds/

data.title: Since the "1011 crash," Tether and Circle have minted $15 billion worth of stablecoins.
data.description: According to Lookonchain, Circle has just minted $500 million worth of USDC. Since the "10/11 crash," Tether and Circle have minted stablecoins worth $15 billion.

data.title: ETH Tests $2.8K Support, Tom Lee Declares Bottom Is In – Potential $7K Breakout Ahead?
data.description:
Ethereum rebounds from $2,870 support as Fundstrat's Tom Lee declares the bottom is in, projecting a breakout toward $7,000 by Q1 2026 driven by BlackRock's staked ETF filing and institutional accumulation.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/eth-tests-2-8k-support-tom-lee-declares-bottom-in-ahead-of-potential-7k-breakout/

data.title: 21Shares launched six cryptocurrency ETPs, including AAVE and LINK, on ​​Nasdaq Stockholm.
data.description: Cryptocurrency exchange-traded product (ETP) issuer 21shares announced the launch of six cryptocurrency ETPs on Nasdaq Stockholm: Aave ETP, Cardano ETP, Chainlink ETP, Polkadot ETP, Crypto Basket Index ETP, and Crypto Basket 10 Core ETP. (Globenewswire)

data.title: BlackRock transferred 14,513 ETH, worth $43.7 million, to Coinbase.
data.description: According to Arkham's monitoring, approximately 54 minutes ago, BlackRock transferred a total of 14,513 ETH, worth $43.7 million, to a Coinbase Prime address.

data.title: Strategy and Harvard Increase Bitcoin Holdings Amid Market Volatility
data.description: According to PANews, Strategy has acquired 8,178 bitcoins for $835.6 million, with an average purchase price of $102,171 per bitcoin. Despite the current price falling below $90,000, resulting in a paper loss for this batch, the company's overall average cost remains at $74,433, keeping it profitable. It is estimated that 40% of its holdings were acquired below this cost. Harvard Management Company disclosed in its September 30 13F filing that it holds 6.8 million shares of BlackRock's Bitcoin ETF (IBIT), valued at $442.9 million, marking a 200% increase from the previous quarter. This makes it the highest-valued holding among its U.S.-listed stocks. Managing $50 billion in assets, the institution's increased investment during a downturn highlights its long-term confidence in digital assets. The increased holdings by these two institutions coincide with a significant market adjustment: financing rates have turned negative, open interest has declined, and short-term holders (wallets acquiring coins within 155 days) have experienced 'on-chain capitulation.' Retail investors have been selling off due to leveraged liquidations and realized losses. The U.S. spot Bitcoin ETF's monthly market value shrank by $2.57 billion, marking the largest drop since its inception, with outflows concentrated during U.S. trading hours, further pressuring prices. This pattern of retail sell-offs and institutional buying reflects a typical transfer of funds from weaker to stronger investors. On-chain data shows that whale wallets holding over 1,000 bitcoins have continued to accumulate as smaller wallets exit, consistent with early-stage fund redistribution during historical pullbacks. However, it is important to note that wallet labeling relies on blockchain forensics and exchange tags, lacking KYC verification, which limits the accuracy of holding data. CryptoQuant data indicates a deleveraging trend in the derivatives market, with declining open interest and negative funding rates, suggesting more long-term position closures rather than active whale withdrawals. Despite the significant institutional purchases by Strategy and Harvard, they are insufficient to offset the $2.57 billion ETF redemption pressure. If ETF outflows persist until the end of the year or macroeconomic risks escalate, Bitcoin's clearing price could further decline, even with increased holdings by sovereign states and corporations. Strategy's financing strategy allows it to dilute costs over the long term, and Harvard's investment horizon spans a decade, making quarterly pullbacks less impactful. However, retail and leveraged traders lack such buffers. The ultimate nature of this fund redistribution remains uncertain: if ETF outflows stabilize and institutional spot demand continues, it may signal a bottom. Conversely, if outflows and macro pressures intensify, the current accumulation may only be a temporary respite. Bitcoin's drop below $90,000 has filtered out long-term investors who can withstand volatility from speculators sensitive to short-term fluctuations, with the final outcome to be revealed in the coming month's fund flows.

data.title: Grayscale: Grayscale Sui Trust has started trading on OTCQX
data.description: Digital asset management company Grayscale announced on its X platform that the Grayscale Sui Trust (GSUI) has begun trading on OTCQX, with a reported trading fee of 2.50%.

data.title: BOB airdrop claims are now open.
data.description: Bitcoin DeFi project BOB announced on the X platform that its airdrop application is now open. Previously, it was reported that the initial airdrop of BOB will account for 4.15% of the total supply, with 2.15% allocated to eligible addresses that have participated in the BOB ecosystem and other activities, and the remaining 2% as staking rewards.

data.title: KindlyMD Reports Bitcoin Holdings and Strategic Investments in Q3
data.description: According to Odaily, KindlyMD, a Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin treasury company, has released its third-quarter financial report. The report reveals that following its merger with Nakamoto, the company holds 5,398 Bitcoins as of November 12, 2025. Additionally, 367 Bitcoins have been allocated for strategic investments. This month, KindlyMD invested $6 million in Swiss Bitcoin fund management company FUTURE Holdings AG.

data.title: Whale Shifts Strategy After Losses in Ethereum and Bitcoin
data.description: According to Foresight News, Lookonchain has reported that a major cryptocurrency investor, identified by the address starting with 0x0ddf, has changed their trading strategy following significant losses. After closing long positions in Ethereum (ETH) and Bitcoin (BTC) with a loss of approximately $6.2 million, the investor has now adopted a bearish stance. They have opened a short position with 2x leverage on 20,000 ETH, valued at around $60.4 million.

data.title: A whale closed its long positions in ETH and BTC, incurring a loss of $6.2 million. After opening short positions, it has yet to turn a profit.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, a whale closed its long positions in ETH and BTC, incurring a loss of $6.2 million. It then opened a short position in Ethereum with 2x leverage and currently holds 20,000 ETH, with a floating loss of $60.4 million.

data.title: BitMine Faces Significant Losses on Ethereum Holdings
data.description:
According to PANews, a recent report by 10x Research reveals that BitMine, the largest corporate holder of Ethereum globally, is experiencing substantial losses. The report indicates that BitMine is losing $1,000 per Ethereum compared to its average cost, resulting in a total unrealized loss of $3.7 billion on its Ethereum holdings.

The report highlights that the decline in the net asset value (NAV) of corporate crypto treasury companies is making it challenging to attract new retail investors. Many existing shareholders are effectively 'trapped' unless they sell at significantly reduced prices. As premiums inevitably drop to zero, investors may find themselves stuck in this structure, unable to exit without incurring substantial losses. Unlike ETFs, digital asset treasury companies (DATs) have complex, opaque, and often hedge fund-like fee structures that quietly erode returns.

Data from Bitminetracker shows that BitMine's basic mNAV is 0.77, with a diluted mNAV of 0.92. BitMine holds approximately 3.56 million ETH, valued at around $10.7 billion, accounting for 2.94% of the total Ethereum supply. The company's average cost basis is $4,051 per ETH. Other digital asset treasury companies, including Strategy, Bitmine, Metaplanet, Sharplink Gaming, Upexi, and DeFi Development Corp, have also seen significant declines in their mNAV.

data.title: A newly created wallet withdrew 5,000 ETH from Binance in the past four hours.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, in the past 4 hours, the newly created wallet 0x499f withdrew 5,000 ETH (worth $15.04 million) from Binance.

data.title: KindlyMD Financial Report: Holding 5,398 Bitcoins and allocating 367 Bitcoins for strategic investments.
data.description: Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin treasury company KindlyMD released its Q3 financial report, disclosing that following its merger with Nakamoto, as of November 12, 2025, the company held 5,398 bitcoins and had allocated 367 bitcoins for strategic investments. Additionally, it invested $6 million this month in Swiss Bitcoin fund management company FUTURE Holdings AG. (Businesswire)

data.title: The Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission (SFC) is warning the public to be wary of suspicious investment products such as Goldpay Token and Gold Receipt.
data.description: The Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission (SFC) is warning the public to be wary of suspicious investment products called “Goldpay Token (GPT)” and “Gold Receipt (GR)”. These Hong Kong products involve digital tokens linked to physical gold, and they are not authorized by the SFC to be offered to the public.

data.title: U.S. Stock Market Sees Surge in Insider Buying Amid Decline
data.description: According to Odaily, the U.S. stock market has experienced its worst decline since April, sparking a buying spree among corporate insiders. Over the past 30 days, executives of publicly traded companies have been purchasing their own company stocks at the fastest rate since May. This activity comes amid market panic, driven by concerns over an artificial intelligence bubble, which has led investors to shift from overvalued tech stocks to more defensive sectors. The surge in insider buying has provided some comfort to bullish investors, as the S&P 500 index recently fell by 3.1% in a week, heading towards its worst month since April. These executives have been buying while other 'bottom fishers' remain hesitant, as the U.S. stock market has faced five consecutive days of decline, with intraday rebounds quickly erased.

data.title: UBS Global Wealth: S&P 500 Index Could Reach 7700 Points by the End of 2026
data.description: Strategists at UBS Global Wealth Management believe the S&P 500 will reach 7,700 points by the end of 2026, a 16% increase from current levels. This growth will be driven by the "Big Seven" stocks and the accelerating impact of artificial intelligence. The team, including Mark Haefele, stated that strong consumer demand, loose monetary policy, and fiscal support will underpin this outlook.
